description: mcpcc: gcc/clang wrapper that emits an MCP tool bundle (mcp.json) and a stdio MCP server for linked executables.

userStories.title: Bootstrap Rust workspace and binaries
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Rust workspace with `mcpcc` and `mcpcc-mcp-server` binaries so that the rest of the implementation has a stable foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Cargo workspace with two binaries: `mcpcc` and `mcpcc-mcp-server`
Binaries build successfully with `cargo build`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Parse mcpcc wrapper flags and select underlying compiler
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `mcpcc` to separate wrapper flags from compiler/linker flags and pick the correct underlying compiler so that existing build commands work unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: Support `mcpcc [mcpcc_flags...] -- [compiler_flags...]` and also a no-`--` mode where only `--mcpcc-*` are treated as wrapper flags
Implement compiler selection precedence: `--mcpcc-cc` > `MCPCC_CC` env > `CC` env > `clang` if available > `gcc`
Add `mcpcc --mcpcc-print-cc` to print the resolved underlying compiler path and exit 0 without compiling
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Passthrough compilation and exit-code contract
userStories.description: As a build-system user, I want `mcpcc` to invoke the underlying compiler with identical arguments and propagate exit codes so that builds behave like plain gcc/clang.
acceptanceCriteria: Invoke the resolved underlying compiler with the passthrough args (preserving ordering)
If the underlying compiler exits non-zero, `mcpcc` exits with the same code and does not generate MCP artifacts
Wrapper CLI usage errors exit with code 2
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Detect executable link and resolve artifact paths
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `mcpcc` to only generate MCP artifacts when linking an executable and to compute deterministic output paths so that results are predictable and cacheable.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect compile-only modes (`-c`, `-E`, `-S`, `-shared`) and skip MCP artifact generation for those invocations
Resolve `bin_path` from `-o <path>` or default to `./a.out` when linking
Implement default artifact naming: `base.mcp.json`, `base.mcp-server`, `base.mcpcc-manifest.json` in `dirname(bin_path)`
Support output overrides: `--mcpcc-artifacts-dir`, `--mcpcc-mcp-json-out`, `--mcpcc-server-out`, `--mcpcc-manifest-out`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write minimal mcp.json with required fallback tool (atomic)
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want every linked executable build to produce a valid tool bundle with a guaranteed raw runner tool so that the binary can always be executed via MCP.
acceptanceCriteria: When linking an executable, write `<base>.mcp.json` containing `mcpccVersion`, `mcpSpecVersion=2025-11-25`, `binary.path`, and a non-empty `tools[]`
Always include a fallback tool named `<base>.run_raw` with a non-null JSON Schema object `inputSchema`
Use atomic write (temp file, validate JSON parse, then rename) for `mcp.json`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Manifest, logging, and MCP-generation failure exit code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want deterministic artifacts and actionable logs (plus a manifest) so that failures are easy to debug and CI can distinguish compilation from MCP-generation errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Write `<base>.mcpcc-manifest.json` with compiler argv/exitCode, artifact paths, extractor/analysis metadata, and LLM metadata placeholders
Default logs are short; `--mcpcc-verbose` prints detailed decisions and artifact paths to stderr
If compilation succeeds but MCP artifact generation fails, `mcpcc` exits with code 70
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: OpenRouter LLM descriptions with cache and modes
userStories.description: As a user, I want `mcpcc` to generate short tool/param descriptions via OpenRouter (with caching) so that MCP tools are self-describing while remaining deterministic across rebuilds.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` auth and call OpenRouter using an OpenAI-compatible API with `temperature=0`
Implement `--mcpcc-llm-mode required|best-effort|off` with default `required`; `off` is only allowed when `MCPCC_ALLOW_NO_LLM=1` is set
Cache key is SHA-256(`promptVersion` + `model` + `analysis_summary_json`) and cache files are written to `cache_dir/llm/<hash>.json`
In `required` mode, cache miss triggers an API call; API failure causes `mcpcc` to exit 70
In `best-effort` mode, missing key/API failure produces deterministic placeholder descriptions and is recorded in the manifest
Model output is strict JSON with `toolDescription` and `params` string map; enforce 5–240 char trimmed descriptions and ignore unknown keys
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: getopt_long extractor → structured tool schema + argv mapping
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want a structured tool for `getopt_long`-based CLIs so that arguments can be called with a JSON object instead of raw argv.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse `struct option long_options[]` to extract long option name + has_arg (no/required/optional) and candidate short option from `val` when it is a char literal
Parse the `optstring` passed to `getopt_long` to determine short option arg requirements (`a`, `a:`, `a::`)
When extraction succeeds, generate a structured tool named `<base>` with `inputSchema.additionalProperties=false`, extracted option properties, and `args: string[]` for remaining positionals
Generate deterministic `x-mcpcc.argvMapping.options[]` ordering matching initializer order
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: argp extractor → structured tool schema + argv mapping
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want a structured tool for `argp`-based CLIs so that common Linux CLIs become callable with typed JSON input.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse `struct argp_option[]` initializers to extract long name, short key (char literal), arg requirement, and doc string
Treat `OPTION_ARG_OPTIONAL` as an optional-argument option
When extraction succeeds, generate a structured tool named `<base>` with `inputSchema.additionalProperties=false`, extracted option properties, and `args: string[]` for remaining positionals
Generate deterministic `x-mcpcc.argvMapping.options[]` ordering matching initializer order
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Annotation DSL header + ELF section reader + deterministic merge
userStories.description: As a power user, I want to override tool/param metadata deterministically via annotations so that heuristics can be corrected without changing extraction code.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `mcpcc_annot.h` that embeds JSON strings in a `.mcpcc` section using `MCPCC_TOOL_JSON(...)` and `MCPCC_PARAM_JSON(...)` macros
Read `.mcpcc` section data from the linked executable and parse `MCPCC_TOOL:` / `MCPCC_PARAM:` JSON payloads
Merge precedence is deterministic: annotation > argp/getopt > fallback
Annotation can override tool title/description and exec limits (timeout/max stdout/stderr) and can override param mapping and descriptions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Finalize tool bundle: structured + raw, exec defaults, schema rules
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want a complete and valid tool bundle that always works (raw) and is structured when possible so that tool calling is reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Extractor selection order is: annotations (merge) then argp then getopt_long then fallback-only
If a structured tool is generated, `mcp.json.tools[]` includes both `<base>` and `<base>.run_raw`; otherwise only `<base>.run_raw`
Set default exec limits: `timeoutMs=30000`, `maxStdoutBytes=1048576`, `maxStderrBytes=1048576` and allow annotation overrides
Ensure every tool has non-null `inputSchema` and tool names match `[a-zA-Z0-9._-]` and length 1–128
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement generic mcpcc-mcp-server: stdio initialize + listTools
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want a stdio MCP server binary that reads `*.mcp.json` and lists tools so that clients can discover available tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement MCP stdio server (`stdin`/`stdout`, JSON-RPC 2.0) compatible with MCP Spec 2025-11-25 tools capability
Server reads the adjacent `<base>.mcp.json` at runtime and serves `tools/listTools` from its `tools[]` array
Server supports MCP lifecycle: `initialize` then `initialized` before serving tool calls
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: mcpcc emits the per-binary server executable (copy)
userStories.description: As a user, I want each linked executable build to produce a runnable `<base>.mcp-server` so that I can launch an MCP server for that binary without rebuilding Rust each time.
acceptanceCriteria: On successful executable link, copy the packaged `mcpcc-mcp-server` binary to `<base>.mcp-server` (or `--mcpcc-server-out` override)
Server copy is skipped when MCP generation is skipped (compile-only modes) or when the underlying compiler fails
Manifest records the final server path
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server: tools/callTool for run_raw (spawn + limits + structuredContent)
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want to run the target binary via `<base>.run_raw` and get stdout/stderr/exitCode back so that the tool is usable in automation.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `tools/callTool` for `<base>.run_raw` to spawn the target binary and return `stdout`, `stderr`, `exitCode`, `durationMs`, `timedOut`, `truncatedStdout`, `truncatedStderr` in `structuredContent`
Enforce timeout and stdout/stderr byte limits (defaults from `x-mcpcc.exec`, with annotation overrides reflected in `mcp.json`)
`isError` is true on spawn error, timeout, or non-zero exit code
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server: structured tool calling (input validation + argv mapping)
userStories.description: As an MCP client, I want to call the structured tool with a JSON object and have it mapped deterministically to argv so that CLIs are invoked safely and predictably.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate structured tool input objects with `additionalProperties:false` and return an `isError=true` tool result for unknown properties
Map options into argv using `x-mcpcc.argvMapping.options[]` order, then append `args[]` positionals in provided order
Optional-arg options: omitted → not emitted; empty string → emit flag only; non-empty string → emit with value
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Golden examples + end-to-end integration tests
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a reproducible test suite with example C programs so that regressions in extraction, artifact generation, and server behavior are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Add example C programs covering: argp, getopt_long, no-parser (raw only), and annotation override
Add integration tests that compile examples via `mcpcc` and verify generated `*.mcp.json`, `*.mcp-server`, and `*.mcpcc-manifest.json`
Add server integration tests that perform stdio `initialize`, `tools/listTools`, and at least one `tools/callTool` call and verify `structuredContent` fields
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Documentation (README + SPEC_mcp.json.md)
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear documentation for `mcpcc` usage and the generated `mcp.json` contract so that I can adopt and troubleshoot the tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `README` covering installation/build, wrapper usage examples, environment variables, and artifact outputs
Add `SPEC_mcp.json.md` describing the `mcp.json` format and schema rules (based on PRD section 8)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
